info.title: Outvoicer API
info.description:
API for sending invoices as email or e-invoice and forwarding them to accounting software under sales invoices.

**Prerequisites** (initial setup needs human help; SmartID / Mobile-ID authentication proves the user is a board member of the company before e-invoicing can be enabled):
- Company is registered in Outvoicer.
- If needed: accounting software integration is set up in the web environment.
- If needed: e-invoice is turned on in the web environment.

**Domains**: each customer has their own subdomain, e.g. `https://mydomain.outvoicer.com`. All business endpoints are called against the customer subdomain. Bearer-token management endpoints are called against `https://app.outvoicer.com`.

**Return values**: endpoints were originally designed for JS frontend clients. When data is POSTed or PUT, the successful response always returns the full new current state of the data as an object.

**Duplicate prevention**: endpoints with consequences (sending an invoice to a customer, forwarding an invoice to the ERP) have a duplicate check so they can only be triggered once.

**Numbers vs strings**: `VATRate` is always a JSON number (e.g. 21 or 21.5). Other numeric fields (`unitPrice`, `amount`, `rounding`, `subtotal`, `total`, `VAT`) may arrive as numbers or numeric strings (e.g. 3, 3.63, "3.63", 13.21488), because some workflows import data without recalculating.

subdomain.description: Your company's Outvoicer subdomain. One user (Bearer token) can have access to multiple subdomains.

tags.name: Client Integration
tags.description: Client endpoints that talk to the connected accounting software. Not all accounting softwares support all endpoints.

tags.name: Product Integration
tags.description: Product endpoints that talk to the connected accounting software

tags.name: Invoice
tags.description: Create, print, send and query sales invoices

tags.name: Invoice Integration
tags.description: Forward invoices to the connected accounting software

get.summary: View your Bearer token
get.description: Every user can have one Bearer token. Called against app.outvoicer.com, not the customer subdomain.

post.description: Generates (or regenerates) the user's single Bearer token. Called against app.outvoicer.com.

get.summary: Test the token / connectivity against a customer subdomain

200.description: Token is valid for this subdomain

get.summary: Get all clients

post.summary: Create a client or overwrite it if it already exists
post.description: Recommended in automated workflows: overwrite the client every month, because the e-mail address or company name may have changed. Returns the full updated client (including `client.id`, which is needed for forwarding).

post.summary: Create/overwrite a client and forward it to the accounting software in one call
post.description: Express variant of post-or-overwrite that also forwards the client to the connected accounting software. In highly automated workflows a 2-step approach (post-or-overwrite, then forward) is recommended, because either step can fail independently and separate calls make debugging easier. Returns the full updated client.

get.summary: Get one client

delete.summary: Delete one key from a client
delete.description: Removes a single field (key) from the client document (the data store is NoSQL).

parameters.name: keyname
parameters.description: Name of the client field to delete, e.g. `finvoice` or `discount`

get.summary: Get the client's current e-invoice eligibility / delivery channel

200.description: Current delivery channel information for the client

put.summary: Refresh / verify that the client can receive e-invoices
put.description: Checks e-invoice registries and updates the client's delivery channel so invoices can be delivered as e-invoices where possible.

get.summary: Search clients by name

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Name (or part of a name) to search for

get.summary: Get all invoices of a client

post.summary: Forward a client to the accounting software
post.description: Sends the client to the connected accounting software so it can be referenced on forwarded sales invoices. Payload is an empty object.

get.summary: Import all customers from the accounting software
get.description: Not all accounting softwares have all integration endpoints available.

get.summary: View all clients in the accounting software

200.description: Clients as stored in the accounting software

get.summary: View one client in the accounting software

200.description: Client as stored in the accounting software

delete.summary: Delete a client from the accounting software integration

get.summary: Get client debt or prepayment (balance)
Get all products
Get one product

put.description: Also used for one-time settings such as locking the price so mobile app users cannot change the API-set price: `{ "lockPrice": true }`.

post.summary: Forward a product to the accounting software

get.summary: Import all products from the accounting software
Get seller company settings

200.description: Full current state of the company
Full current state of the company

put.description: Partial update — send only the fields you want to change. Example: set an invoice number prefix to avoid duplicate invoice numbers in the accounting software: { "prefix": "api" }.

200.description: Full current state of the company

get.summary: Query all invoices

post.description: Saves the invoice data. No invoice number or PDF is generated yet (that happens on first print). Requires that the referenced client and products exist and are already forwarded to the accounting software (when integration forwarding is used later).

post.summary: Create an invoice, assign an invoice number and generate the PDF (+ JPG preview)
Create an invoice, generate the PDF and send it to the client
post.description: Sends as e-invoice if the client can receive one, otherwise emails it. Duplicate-protected.

post.summary: Create an invoice, generate the PDF, send it to the client and forward it to the accounting software
post.description: One-shot convenience endpoint. Requires that the referenced client and products exist and have already been forwarded to the accounting software. Duplicate-protected.

get.summary: View one invoice
get.description: The invoice can be addressed by its internal id (e.g. `68b544f233f9e94dcfc24dd3`) at any time, or by its invoice number (e.g. `1234`) once the number has been assigned (numbers are assigned when the PDF is first generated).

get.summary: Create the invoice PDF and assign an invoice number
get.description: The invoice can be printed many times; the invoice number is assigned only on the first print.

post.summary: Send an invoice to the client
post.description: Automatically sends an e-invoice if the client can receive one, otherwise defaults to email. Duplicate-protected: can be triggered only once.

post.summary: Send an invoice by email only
post.description: Duplicate-protected: can be triggered only once.

post.summary: Send an invoice as an e-invoice only
post.description: Requires e-invoicing to be enabled for the seller and the client to be able to receive e-invoices. Duplicate-protected: can be triggered only once.

post.summary: Forward an invoice to the accounting software under sales invoices
post.description: Duplicate-protected: can be triggered only once.

get.summary: Query invoices from a specific month
Get the invoice preview as a JPG
get.description: The JPG preview is generated together with the PDF (post-and-print or print).

get.summary: Download the invoice PDF
View all products in the accounting software
get.description: Not all accounting softwares have all integration endpoints available.

200.description: Products as stored in the accounting software

get.summary: View one product in the accounting software

200.description: Product as stored in the accounting software

delete.summary: Delete product from the accounting software integration

get.summary: View all invoices in the accounting software
get.description: Not all accounting softwares have all integration endpoints available.

200.description: Invoices as stored in the accounting software

get.summary: View one invoice in the accounting software

200.description: Invoice as stored in the accounting software

delete.summary: Delete invoice from the accounting software integration

bearerAuth.description: Every user can have one Bearer token. One user can have access to different subdomains. Manage the token via /api/user/bearer/* on app.outvoicer.com.

InvoiceId.name: id
InvoiceId.description: Invoice internal id (e.g. `68b544f233f9e94dcfc24dd3`) or invoice number (e.g. `1234`; assigned when the PDF is first generated).

ClientResponse.description: Full new current state of the client

ProductResponse.description: Full new current state of the product

InvoiceResponse.description: Full new current state of the invoice

BadRequest.description: Invalid payload (e.g. email fails validation, country is not a real country name, referenced client/product does not exist)

Unauthorized.description: Missing or invalid Bearer token, or the token has no access to this subdomain

Duplicate.description: Duplicate prevented — this consequence-bearing action (send / forward) has already been triggered for this invoice

NotSupportedByIntegration.description: The connected accounting software does not support this integration endpoint

NumberOrNumericString.description: Numeric value that may arrive as a JSON number or as a numeric string, e.g. 3, 3.63, "3.63" or 13.21488. The most normalised form is "3.00", but several workflows (including import-only flows that don't recalculate) are supported.

token.description: The Bearer token to use in the Authorization header

DeliveryType.description: How the invoice is delivered to the client. Unknown values default to Email.

Finvoice.description: Finnish e-invoice (Finvoice) address of the client

EstonianEinvoiceAddress.description: Estonian e-invoice channel address of the client

ClientDelivery.description: Client's current invoice delivery channel / e-invoice eligibility

ClientInput.description: Client payload for POST /client, PUT /client/{clientId}, POST /client/post-or-overwrite and POST /client/post-forward-and-overwrite

email.description: E-mail where the invoice is sent to; must validate

country.description: Must be a real country name for VAT to validate

discount.description: Customer discount as a percentage; number or numeric string

language.description: Language the customer receives invoices in (e.g. `lv`, `et`, `fi`, `en`)

Client.description: Full client object as returned by the API. The data store is NoSQL, so extra fields may be present (see `additionalProperty` in the example), and some legacy fields may have mixed types.

taxVAT.description: Legacy data may contain boolean, string ("true"/"false") or number (0/1); semantically a boolean.

discount.description: Customer discount as a percentage; may be a number or numeric string

meritId.description: Id of the client in the connected accounting software (e.g. Merit)

ProductInput.description: Product payload for POST /product and PUT /product/{productId}

debit.description: Sales revenue account in accounting for this product

VATRate.description: Always a JSON number, e.g. 21 or 21.5. Overwrites the default VAT rate for this product.

Product.description: Full product object as returned by the API. The data store is NoSQL, so extra fields may be present (see `additionalProperty` in the example).

debit.description: Sales revenue account in accounting for this product

VATRate.description: Always a JSON number, e.g. 21 or 21.5. Overwrites the default VAT rate for this product.

LineException.description: Explains why a certain VAT rate was chosen for a line. `false` or `null` means the invoice was imported from elsewhere and this system is not used.

InvoiceLineInput.description: Invoice line when creating an invoice. `product` must reference an existing product id.

InvoiceLine.description: Invoice line as returned by the API

VATRate.description: Always a JSON number, e.g. 21 or 21.5

Seller.description: Seller (your company) block embedded in an invoice; also the shape returned by PUT /company

prefix.description: Invoice number prefix; useful to avoid duplicate number clashes in the accounting software

CompanyUpdate.description: Seller company settings. All fields are optional — send only the ones you want to set/update (e.g. just { "prefix": "api" }). The data store is NoSQL, so extra fields are allowed.

prefix.description: Invoice number prefix; useful to avoid duplicate invoice numbers in the accounting software

InvoiceCreate.description: Payload for creating an invoice. Requires that the referenced client and product ids exist (and, for forwarding endpoints, are already sent to the accounting software).

Invoice.description: Full sales invoice as returned by the API. The data store is NoSQL, so extra fields may be present (see `additionalProperty` in the example).

id.description: Internal id; usable immediately after creation

nr.description: Invoice number; assigned when the PDF is first generated. May be returned as a string or a number.

date.description: Invoice date as a Unix timestamp in milliseconds (string)

due.description: Due date as a Unix timestamp in milliseconds (string)
